title: Fix overflow indexing in causal_conv1d kernel
body:
## Essential Elements of an Effective PR Description Checklist
- [x] The purpose of the PR, such as "Fix some issue (link existing issues this PR will resolve)".
- [x] The test plan, such as providing test command.
- [x] The test results, such as pasting the results comparison before and after, or e2e results
- [x] (Optional) The necessary documentation update, such as updating `supported_models.md` and `examples` for a new model.

## Purpose

This PR fixes an indexing overflow issue that can occur when running mamba-based models in V1. Before V1 integration, the value of `conv_state_batch_coord` could vary from `0` to `max_num_seqs-1`. However, with V1 integration it can now vary between `0` and `num_gpu_blocks-1`. Depending on the model and deployment, the number of blocks can be orders of magnitude higher and I'm running into overflow issues unless we explicitly cast to `tl.int64`.

Please note I already made the [equivalent fix](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/blob/main/vllm/model_executor/layers/mamba/ops/mamba_ssm.py#L111) to the `selective_state_update` as part of https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/20016. I forgot to make the equivalent change to this kernel, my bad. 

@tlrmchlsmth @cyang49 @thoangtrvn 

## Test Plan

It is actually not that easy to catch this with a test because you need a lot of blocks and need to let it run for a while until the scheduler starts picking off blocks with a high enough index to cause the overflow. I think it could be good to write some tests to try and catch these kernel overflow issues in general, but I would advocate for getting this fix in now. 

## Test Result

n/a

## (Optional) Documentation Update

n/a

<!--- pyml disable-next-line no-emphasis-as-heading -->

step_plan.summary: Cast conv_state_batch_coord to tl.int64 in both Triton kernels (_causal_conv1d_fwd_kernel and _causal_conv1d_update_kernel) to prevent 32-bit integer overflow in pointer/index arithmetic introduced by larger index ranges under V1.

steps.operation: Cast conv_state_batch_coord to tl.int64 in forward kernel where it is loaded or derived
steps.reason: conv_state_batch_coord is used in pointer/index arithmetic (multiplied by stride_conv_state_seq and used to compute conv_states_base). Under V1 the index range (num_gpu_blocks) can be much larger and can overflow 32-bit arithmetic. Explicitly casting the loaded value (from conv_state_indices_ptr) and the idx_seq-derived value to tl.int64 prevents integer overflow and matches the earlier fix in mamba_ssm.selective_state_update.
steps.side_effects: Behavioral semantics unchanged except integer width for index arithmetic. Tests that exercise causal_conv1d should continue to pass; add/verify kernel unit tests if available. Downstream code that depends on exact integer dtypes in Triton kernels is unlikely to be affected because indexing operations remain logically the same; Triton supports comparisons across integer widths but confirm no implicit assumptions about tl.int32 elsewhere. No external API change. Slightly larger register usage in kernel may occur but is negligible.

steps.operation: Cast conv_state_batch_coord to tl.int64 in update kernel where it is loaded or derived
steps.reason: Same root cause as the forward kernel: conv_state_batch_coord can exceed 32-bit range under V1 and is used to compute conv_states_base and masks. Casting to tl.int64 when reading from conv_state_indices_ptr or when deriving from idx_seq ensures pointer arithmetic does not overflow and keeps behavior consistent with the fix applied to mamba_ssm.selective_state_update.
steps.side_effects: No semantic changes beyond preventing overflow. Existing tests should still pass; consider running tests/kernels/mamba/test_causal_conv1d.py. Potential small impact on kernel resource usage (registers / instruction mix). Ensure comparisons against pad_slot_id and num_cache_lines behave as intended when mixing integer widths (Triton supports this, but validate). No external API changes; downstream callers are unaffected.
